title: Build an Automation (Smart Flow)
lede: Use the visual Automation builder to connect a trigger to one or more actions - so tasks like creating a lead, sending a message, or starting a call happen automatically.

steps.title: Open Automation
steps.content:
1. In the left menu, go to Intelligence Center → Automation.
  2. This opens the visual workflow dashboard where all of your automations live.
  3. Click New Flow (or the + button) to start a fresh automation.

images.caption: The Automation dashboard lists your workflows.
The Automation dashboard lists your workflows.

steps.title: Choose a Trigger
steps.content:
1. Every flow starts with one trigger - the event that kicks it off.
  2. Click the first block and pick a trigger. A common one is Catch Webhook, which gives you a URL that external systems can send data to.
  3. Other triggers include a schedule (run every X minutes) or an app event.

images.caption: Pick a trigger, then chain actions below it.
Pick a trigger, then chain actions below it.

steps.title: Add Actions
steps.content:
1. Click the + below the trigger to add an action - the task that runs.
  2. Use the System Tables actions to work with iMash data: create a lead or contact, look up rows, or start an outbound call.
  3. Reference data from the trigger (for example a phone number that arrived in the webhook) inside each action's fields.
  4. Add as many actions as you need - they run top to bottom.

steps.title: Test and Publish
steps.content:
1. Use Test to run the flow with sample data and confirm each step behaves.
  2. Fix any step that shows an error before going live.
  3. Toggle the flow On (Publish) so it runs automatically from now on.

nextSteps.title: What's Next?

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Create automated workflows in iMash: pick a trigger like Catch Webhook, add System Tables actions, then test and publish your flow.
